appraisal_events.evidence: « une vie crapuleuse racontée par la déchéance morale »; « le vice ... débordait maintenant dans ses propos »; description des joues fardées, bajoues, croupe rebondie, prêtre interdit.
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator insists on Charlus’s moral and physical decline, visible on his face and his body, and on the way his vice now also manifests itself in his words.

appraisal_events.evidence: « il reconnaissait immédiatement ce à quoi personne n’eût jamais fait attention »; « je crois que si baron de Charlus eût tâté de la prose… l’homme du monde fût devenu maître écrivain »; « quel dictionnaire délicieux, quel répertoire inépuisable ! »
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator elevates Charlus by praising his eye, his knowledge of details and of names, and by regretting that he did not write, going so far as to judge him a potentially great writer.

status_effects.explanation: His local image is degraded by the emphatic portrait of a moral and physical decadence now readable and invasive.
He gains aesthetic and intellectual authority via the praise of his sense of observation and his potential capacity as a writer.
